# Accordion

Exclusive open, animated height auto, correct semantics, find-in-page support. The browser does all of it.

```html
<div class="w-full max-w-md [interpolate-size:allow-keywords]">
  <details
    name="acc-faq"
    open
    class="group border-b border-border details-content:h-0 details-content:overflow-clip details-content:transition-[height,content-visibility] details-content:transition-discrete details-content:duration-250 details-content:ease-out open:details-content:h-auto"
  >
    <summary class="flex cursor-pointer list-none items-center justify-between gap-4 rounded-sm py-4 text-sm font-medium hover:underline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden after:size-2.5 after:shrink-0 after:rotate-45 after:border-r-[1.5px] after:border-b-[1.5px] after:border-muted-foreground after:transition-transform after:content-[''] group-open:after:rotate-[225deg]">
      Is it accessible?
    </summary>
    <div class="pb-4 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Yes — <code>&lt;details&gt;</code>/<code>&lt;summary&gt;</code> ship with keyboard support and
      correct semantics, and the content stays in the DOM for crawlers and find-in-page.
    </div>
  </details>
  <details
    name="acc-faq"
    class="group border-b border-border details-content:h-0 details-content:overflow-clip details-content:transition-[height,content-visibility] details-content:transition-discrete details-content:duration-250 details-content:ease-out open:details-content:h-auto"
  >
    <summary class="flex cursor-pointer list-none items-center justify-between gap-4 rounded-sm py-4 text-sm font-medium hover:underline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden after:size-2.5 after:shrink-0 after:rotate-45 after:border-r-[1.5px] after:border-b-[1.5px] after:border-muted-foreground after:transition-transform after:content-[''] group-open:after:rotate-[225deg]">
      Can it animate height: auto?
    </summary>
    <div class="pb-4 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Yes. <code>interpolate-size: allow-keywords</code> on the wrapper lets
      <code>::details-content</code> transition from <code>height: 0</code> to
      <code>height: auto</code>. No measuring, no JavaScript.
    </div>
  </details>
  <details
    name="acc-faq"
    class="group border-b border-border details-content:h-0 details-content:overflow-clip details-content:transition-[height,content-visibility] details-content:transition-discrete details-content:duration-250 details-content:ease-out open:details-content:h-auto"
  >
    <summary class="flex cursor-pointer list-none items-center justify-between gap-4 rounded-sm py-4 text-sm font-medium hover:underline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden after:size-2.5 after:shrink-0 after:rotate-45 after:border-r-[1.5px] after:border-b-[1.5px] after:border-muted-foreground after:transition-transform after:content-[''] group-open:after:rotate-[225deg]">
      Only one open at a time?
    </summary>
    <div class="pb-4 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      The <code>name</code> attribute makes the group exclusive — the browser closes the others
      for you.
    </div>
  </details>
</div>
```

## How it works

- `<details name="acc-faq">` makes the group exclusive — the browser closes the other panels.
- `details-content:h-0` → `open:details-content:h-auto` animates the panel height. The trick that
  makes `height: auto` animatable is `[interpolate-size:allow-keywords]` on the wrapper — it's an
  inherited property, so one wrapper class covers every panel and the snippet is fully
  self-contained (nothing to add to your CSS config).
- `details-content:transition-discrete` (`transition-behavior: allow-discrete`) keeps the content painted (`content-visibility`) while it
  collapses, so the close animation is visible too.
- The chevron is a `after:` pseudo-element rotated with `group-open:`.

## Accessibility

`<details>`/`<summary>` ship keyboard support and disclosure semantics natively, and unlike
`display: none` accordions, collapsed content is still searchable with find-in-page in
Chromium (`content-visibility: hidden` keeps it in the DOM).

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **Exclusive accordions (<details name>)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/details#name))
  Without it: All panels can be open at the same time — still fully functional.
- **::details-content pseudo-element** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/::details-content))
  Without it: Panels open and close instantly, without the height animation.
- **interpolate-size: allow-keywords** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/interpolate-size))
  Without it: height: auto cannot be interpolated, so content snaps open instead of easing.
